opening_message: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.
scenario_description: Borrower starts by giving a realistic payment update, then reveals unemployment, refuses to commit to payment, and resists pressure as the conversation continues.
borrower_profile: Borrower is financially stressed, frustrated, and increasingly resistant.
borrower_intent: Explain they cannot pay right now, avoid committing to payment, and push back if pressured.
stop_condition: Stop after the final notice stage or after the scripted follow-up messages are exhausted.
follow_up_messages: I am unemployed right now and I cannot commit to any payment.
I am not giving dates or agreeing to any plan.
This conversation is going nowhere and I am done discussing payment.

opening_message: I do want to clear this loan, but I cannot pay the full amount today.
scenario_description: Borrower is cooperative and wants to repay, but is dealing with a short-term cash flow constraint after an unexpected family medical expense and delayed salary credit. They can make a small payment now and a larger payment after their next salary date, but they need the agent to work within those limits.
borrower_profile: Borrower is polite, practical, and trying to find a realistic way to pay without overcommitting.
borrower_intent: Show willingness to pay, explain the temporary constraint clearly, avoid making promises they cannot keep, and try to agree to a realistic policy-compliant option such as a structured payment plan if the agent proposes one.
reply_style_rules: Do not agree to impossible dates or amounts.
If the agent offers a realistic plan, show willingness to consider it.
stop_condition: Stop after a clear payment arrangement is reached, or after the system pushes unrealistic terms repeatedly.
expected_path_notes: This should test whether the collections flow can gather the constraint, avoid unnecessary escalation, and move toward a realistic resolution path instead of forcing refusal or final notice.
follow_up_messages: I had a family medical expense this week and my salary is delayed, so I can only pay a small amount right now.
I can pay 2000 now and the rest after my salary comes in on the 10th, but I cannot commit to the full amount before that.
If there is a structured plan that fits that timing, I am willing to discuss it.
